title: Two Tapestry Poems by Lydgate: The Life of St. George and the Falls of Seven Princes (encoded selection)
summary: The repository XML is a pinned copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00085. The U-M encoding represents only printed pp. 13–26 of Eleanor Prescott Hammond's 1910 article: it omits the title and introductory source discussion on pp. 10–12, then retains Hammond's editions of two Lydgate poems, intervening prose with one eight-line coronation-banquet sotelty quotation, and closing commentary. Hammond prints the Legend of St George from Bodleian MS Bodley 686, fols. 200v–204r, collating Trinity R.3.20, pp. 74–81; prints Of the Sodein Fal of Princes in Oure Dayes from Trinity R.3.20, pp. 359–361; and quotes the sotelty stanza from St John's College Oxford MS 57, fol. 220v. DIMEV version 1.0 resolves the Legend's fourth collateral witness as Chetham's Library MS 6709, fols. 193r–198v. Complete public facsimiles of Bodley 686, Trinity R.3.20, Trinity R.3.21, and St John's 57 are cached as whole-manuscript IIIF bundles with exact work maps. Chetham's official page links high-resolution MS 6709 images, but the legacy LUNA inventory is currently behind a verification gate, so its target overlap and whole-codex completeness remain unaudited and uncached. Current British Library records for the collateral Sodein witnesses Harley 2251 and Additional 29729 expose catalog metadata and request routes but no public images. Later DIMEV, MacCracken, and Sponsler evidence clarifies witness loci, attribution, dating, and textual relationships without being projected backward into Hammond's immediate source chain.
review_notes: The encoded cataloging unit is an edited selection, not one medieval work. Part-level scopes prevent the Legend, Sodein Fal, coronation sotelty, and Hammond commentary from inheriting one another's witnesses, dates, form, or rights.
The complete Hammond article occupies printed pp. 10–26 / Internet Archive PDF pp. 18–34. CME00085 begins at printed p. 13 / PDF p. 21 and continues through p. 26 / PDF p. 34.
MacCracken's 1911 manuscript key directly establishes Bodley 686 fols. 200v–204r for the Legend, correcting a provisional range inferred before the edition key was inspected.
DIMEV version 1.0 record 4108 resolves the fourth Legend witness as Chetham's Library MS 6709, fols. 193r–198v. It is a collateral witness and was not an upstream source for Hammond or MacCracken.

Chetham's official page states that MS 6709 was digitized and links high-resolution images, but the link currently reaches a reCAPTCHA-gated LUNA author search. Provider controls were not bypassed; image count, complete physical coverage, target overlap, and terms remain unknown, so no local manuscript cache is claimed.
MacCracken cites Harley 2251 by old foliation 254r–v; the current British Library catalog places the same Sodein Fal item on fol. 228r–v. Both locators belong to one physical witness.
MacCracken places Sodein Fal in Additional 29729 at fols. 169v–170r. The current catalog's adjacent item boundaries are compatible with that locus but do not independently label the poem there.

DIMEV record 813 instead gives Additional 29729 fols. 169r–169v. The manifest retains this conflict and gives MacCracken's direct edition key precedence for the working map pending image comparison or repository confirmation.

Sponsler describes Additional 29729 as Stow's copy of Trinity R.3.20, while the British Library describes the volume more broadly as copied from a lost Shirley manuscript. The copied_from edge is therefore tentative and the description tension remains open.

Hammond identifies the quoted sotelty's codex but gives no folio. Direct inspection of the complete St John's 57 sequence locates the quoted third-course stanza beginning O blessid lady cristis moder dere on fol. 220v, IIIF canvas 452 of 502.
Whole-manuscript caches preserve complete provider sequences rather than cropping to target passages. Each local copy maps the relevant folios, pages, and canvases separately.

Public access and reuse are separate. Bodleian, St John's, and Trinity images carry CC BY-NC 4.0 statements; Chetham/Manchester image terms remain unknown. The project's commercial print-on-demand context requires permission for image redistribution or commercial use even though the medieval texts are public domain.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00085
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00085; the file is 31,881 bytes.
TEMPHEAD records a 2016-09-21 review change, not the original encoding date.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00085
entities.description: A public digital text of Hammond's article pp. 13–26, preserving page breaks, verse groups and lines, commentary, notes, headings, and typographic italics but omitting article pp. 10–12.

entities.label: Eleanor Prescott Hammond, Two Tapestry Poems by Lydgate (1910)
entities.description: Hammond's article includes an introduction on pp. 10–12, an edition of the Legend on pp. 13–21, commentary on pp. 21–23, an eight-line coronation sotelty quotation on p. 23, an edition of Sodein Fal on pp. 23–25, and closing commentary on p. 26.

date_statements.label: Issue publication
date_statements.notes: The volume release statement dates issue 1, pp. 1–160, to the end of December 1910. Later issues completed volume 43 in 1911.

bibliographic.citation: Eleanor Prescott Hammond, 'Two Tapestry Poems by Lydgate: The Life of St. George and the Falls of Seven Princes,' Englische Studien 43 (1910–1911), 10–26.
bibliographic.title: Two Tapestry Poems by Lydgate: The Life of St. George and the Falls of Seven Princes

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Englische Studien volume 43
entities.description: A 492-page PDF scan of the complete volume. Hammond's full article is PDF pp. 18–34; CME00085's selected source pages are PDF pp. 21–34.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 686
entities.description: A 1430s English parchment codex chiefly containing the Canterbury Tales and ten Lydgate poems. The Legend of St George occupies fols. 200v–204r and is Hammond's base witness for that poem.

notes: The catalog localizes the language to south Worcestershire and associates the manuscript with the Beauchamp family and Earls of Warwick.
Canterbury Tales occupies fols. 1r–184v and poems by or attributed to Lydgate fols. 184v–216r.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of MS. Bodl. 686
entities.description: The complete 448-canvas provider sequence, from upper board through manuscript leaves and edges. The Legend maps to fols. 200v–204r, canvases 404–411.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.20
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper anthology written by John Shirley. It contains the Legend at manuscript pp. 74–81 and Sodein Fal at pp. 359–361. Hammond collates its Legend readings and uses it as the base for Sodein Fal.

notes: The catalog attributes the writing to John Shirley and identifies Middle English as the principal vernacular language.

entities.label: Trinity College complete IIIF facsimile of MS R.3.20
entities.description: The complete published 406-canvas sequence. The Legend maps to canvases 92–99 and Sodein Fal to canvases 377–379.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.21
entities.description: A fifteenth-century composite paper codex containing another Legend of St George witness at fols. 314v–317v. It is a collateral witness used in MacCracken's later edition, not a source Hammond reports using.

entities.label: Trinity College complete IIIF facsimile of MS R.3.21
entities.description: The complete published 686-canvas sequence. The Legend maps to fols. 314v–317v, canvases 632–638.

entities.label: Chetham's Library MS 6709
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century manuscript of Lydgate poems, written on vellum and paper. DIMEV record 4108 identifies the fourth Legend of St George witness at fols. 193r–198v; the manuscript was not used by Hammond or MacCracken and belongs to the wider collateral tradition.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: Late fifteenth century; copying reportedly begun in 1480 and completed in 1485
date_statements.notes: Chetham's current page supplies only late fifteenth century. The narrower dates come from contemporary manuscript notes reported in the historical Chetham catalog and should be checked against the codex or a current detailed record.

physical_description.extent: At least 284 folios; exact current collation and furniture not established by the public sources inspected
notes: The historical catalog lists Lydgate's Life of Our Lady, lives of St Cecilia, St Margaret, St George, St Edmund, and St Fremund.
DIMEV maps the Legend exactly to fols. 193r–198v and records the rubric Incipit Vita Sancti Georgii.

entities.label: Rylands Medieval Collection digital images of Chetham's Library MS 6709
entities.description: Chetham's official page states that MS 6709 was digitized and links to high-resolution images in the Rylands Medieval Collection. The link currently expands to a LUNA author search behind a reCAPTCHA verification gate, so the published image inventory, target-folio overlap, and whole-codex completeness could not be audited without bypassing provider controls.
entities.reproduction_format: Legacy LUNA high-resolution image viewer; exact image count and coverage unknown

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2251
entities.description: A London paper anthology dated 1460–1483 and associated with a lost Shirley manuscript. Sodein Fal occupies current fols. 228r–228v, corresponding to old foliation 254r–v cited by MacCracken.

notes: The current catalog records old foliation in the volume; the work's two locators must not be treated as separate copies.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 29729
entities.description: John Stow's mainly autograph paper anthology, copied in 1558 from Shirley-tradition material. MacCracken places Sodein Fal at fols. 169v–170r; the current catalog's adjacent item boundaries are compatible but do not separately label it.

entities.label: St John's College Oxford MS 57
entities.description: An English codex dated 1440–1460 containing a London Chronicle and Chaucer's Parliament of Fowls. The third-course sotelty stanza quoted by Hammond appears on fol. 220v within the coronation-banquet account.

notes: Languages are Middle English and Latin; the catalog localizes the scribal dialect to Essex.
Principal contents include Prick of Conscience, a London Chronicle, Parliament of Fowls, and Military Ordinances.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of St John's College Oxford MS 57
entities.description: The complete 502-canvas provider sequence. The quoted third-course sotelty stanza maps to fol. 220v, canvas 452.

entities.label: MacCracken, The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part I (1911)
entities.description: A later critical edition of religious poems. Its Legend of St George manuscript key identifies Trinity R.3.20 pp. 74–81, Trinity R.3.21 fols. 314v–317v, and Bodley 686 fols. 200v–204r.

bibliographic.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, ed., The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part I, EETS e.s. 107 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1911).
bibliographic.title: The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part I
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 107

entities.label: Internet Archive complete PDF scan of MacCracken Part I
entities.description: A complete PDF reproduction of the 1911 edition, cached locally for comparison with Hammond and the manuscript witnesses.

entities.label: MacCracken, The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part II (1934)
entities.description: A later critical edition of secular poems. Its Sodein Fal manuscript key identifies Trinity R.3.20 pp. 359–361, Harley 2251 old foliation 254r–v, and Additional 29729 fols. 169v–170r.

bibliographic.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, ed., The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part II, EETS o.s. 192 (London: Oxford University Press, 1934).
bibliographic.title: The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part II
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 192

entities.label: Internet Archive complete PDF scan of MacCracken Part II
entities.description: A complete PDF reproduction of the 1934 edition, cached locally for comparison with Hammond and the manuscript witnesses.

entities.label: Claire Sponsler, John Lydgate: Mummings and Entertainments (2010)
entities.description: A modern open-access scholarly edition of Lydgate's performance and display works, including the Legend, Sodein Fal, and coronation sotelties, with introductions and notes on context, date, form, and witnesses.

bibliographic.citation: Claire Sponsler, ed., John Lydgate: Mummings and Entertainments (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2010).
bibliographic.title: John Lydgate: Mummings and Entertainments

entities.label: DIMEV version 1.0 records 4108 and 813
entities.description: Versioned scholarly census records for the Legend of St George (IMEV/NIMEV 2592) and Of the Sodein Fal (IMEV/NIMEV 500). Record 4108 resolves four Legend witnesses including Chetham 6709; record 813 lists the three Sodein witnesses.
notes: DIMEV's R.3.21 locator in record 4108 is internally malformed; the official Trinity catalog and MacCracken control that witness mapping.
DIMEV record 813 gives Additional 29729 as fols. 169r–169v, while MacCracken gives fols. 169v–170r. Both claims remain attributed rather than silently reconciled.

access.access_method: Use Chetham's official Middle English manuscripts page for the current public item-level summary and digital-image link; use DIMEV for the exact Legend locus.
Follow Chetham's official high-resolution-images link. It currently expands to a LUNA author-results URL that requires reCAPTCHA verification before the image inventory can be inspected.
access.format: Legacy LUNA high-resolution image viewer; exact count and coverage unknown
notes: The verification gate was not bypassed. No local image bundle was created, and neither target overlap nor whole-manuscript completeness is asserted.
A future successful access audit must enumerate the complete published object and prove whether fols. 193r–198v are present before recording a work_portion.

access.access_method: Email the librarian or archivist using Chetham's official contact page. Request the complete current physical manuscript—including covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every leaf, and edges where offered—and separately identify the Legend at fols. 193r–198v.
notes: Ask for exact file inventory, reproduction scope, attribution, redistribution, commercial-use, and repository-publication terms before retaining or publishing supplied files.

access.access_method: The current catalog says legacy digital images are unavailable. Request physical access or reproduction through the catalog's enquiry links.
notes: No complete public facsimile was found; do not treat the historical digital-image label as working image access.

access.access_method: Use the British Library catalog request process. Manuscript consultation in London requires the applicable reader registration and a letter of introduction.
The current catalog says legacy digital images are unavailable. Request physical access or reproduction through the catalog's enquiry links.

rights.component: Underlying medieval works by John Lydgate
rights.basis: The medieval works were created centuries before modern copyright and are public domain; this statement does not govern modern transcriptions, editions, images, or provider terms.

rights.component: U-M CME00085 digital text and item materials
rights.basis: The U-M item states that the materials are in the public domain and uses the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.
notes: No Copyright–United States is jurisdiction-specific and must not be generalized to manuscript images or modern editions.

rights.basis: First published in 1910, well before the current United States public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: Hammond's article text and scholarly contributions
rights.basis: Hammond died in 1933; the ordinary UK literary-work term of life plus seventy years expired at the end of 2003, absent a special exception.

rights.component: Internet Archive page-image PDF and access copy
rights.basis: The underlying 1910 article is public domain in the United States, but the Internet Archive item supplies no explicit worldwide reuse license for the scan.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian color images and IIIF derivatives of MS Bodl. 686
rights.basis: The item manifest marks the images copyright Bodleian Libraries and licenses them CC BY-NC 4.0.
The manuscript item and IIIF manifest apply CC BY-NC 4.0; Trinity's Wren Digital Library page separately asks users to seek permission for publication.
notes: The CC license and broader publication-permission policy are preserved separately; commercial repository/POD use requires clarification or permission.

rights.basis: The manuscript item and IIIF manifest apply CC BY-NC 4.0; Trinity's Wren Digital Library page separately asks users to seek permission for publication.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian color images and IIIF derivatives of St John's College Oxford MS 57
rights.basis: The manifest marks the images copyright St John's College Oxford and licenses them CC BY-NC 4.0.
notes: Commercial repository/POD use of the images requires separate permission.

rights.component: Rylands Medieval Collection high-resolution images of Chetham's Library MS 6709
rights.basis: Chetham's official page proves image access but states no item-level license. The gated LUNA inventory and its terms could not be audited, so no redistribution or commercial-use permission is inferred.
notes: Ask Chetham's and the University of Manchester Library for a written credit line, redistribution terms, and commercial/POD permission before retaining or publishing supplied images.

rights.basis: Published in 1911, before the current United States public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: MacCracken Part II edition text and apparatus
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1934. Public download does not establish renewal history or a permissive license; it may remain protected in the United States through 2029 if copyright formalities were satisfied.

rights.component: Sponsler's modern edition, introductions, notes, and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: A 2010 scholarly edition is presumptively copyrighted. Free online access and download do not by themselves authorize republication, and no clear open reuse license was found in the inspected PDF or item pages.

rights.component: DIMEV version 1.0 source data and documentation
rights.basis: The official version 1.0 source archive includes the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International legal code.

editorial_practices.representation: CME00085 begins on printed p. 13 and encodes through p. 26
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item omits the article title and introduction on pp. 10–12 while retaining the two editions and subsequent commentary.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole CME00085 item relative to Hammond's article

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two Legend witnesses with distinct editorial roles
editorial_practices.representation: Continuous Bodley 686 base text with Trinity R.3.20 variants in notes
editorial_practices.description: Hammond does not create one undifferentiated two-manuscript text: Bodley 686 is the continuous Legend base and R.3.20 supplies collation variants; R.3.20 separately serves as the continuous Sodein Fal base.
editorial_practices.scope: Legend, printed pp. 13–21; Sodein Fal, pp. 23–25

editorial_practices.representation: Italic letter sequences within words mark editorial expansions
editorial_practices.description: Hammond uses italics within transcribed word forms to signal expanded abbreviations. Italics elsewhere can also mark titles or emphasis, so interpretation remains contextual rather than a global one-style rule.

editorial_practices.representation: HI elements with italic rendition preserve printed italics
editorial_practices.description: The XML retains print italics, including abbreviation-expansion signals, but does not itself distinguish every functional use of italic type.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source XML for CME00085
evidence.summary: Establishes local identifiers, source structure, page milestones, encoded boundary, wording, commentary, and typographic markup.

evidence.citation: CME00085 structural and repository audit, 12 July 2026
evidence.summary: The file is 31,881 bytes at CME submodule commit e1d77c8 and git blob 62ff7af. Counts are four DIV1, 43 LG, 302 L, 58 MILESTONE, 66 NOTE, eight P, and fourteen PB elements.

evidence.summary: Official 502-canvas sequence establishes complete public image access, canvas labels, attribution to St John's College, and CC BY-NC 4.0 terms.

evidence.citation: St John's College MS 57 coronation-banquet locus audit, 12 July 2026
evidence.summary: Direct inspection identifies the third-course eight-line stanza quoted by Hammond, beginning O blessid lady cristis moder dere, on fol. 220v.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, ed., The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part I (1911)
evidence.locator: Legend of St George manuscript key and edition; printed p. 145
evidence.summary: Later critical edition directly identifies Trinity R.3.20 pp. 74–81, Trinity R.3.21 fols. 314v–317v, and Bodley 686 fols. 200v–204r.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive complete PDF of MacCracken Part I
evidence.summary: Exact complete PDF used for direct manuscript-key and edition inspection.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, ed., The Minor Poems of John Lydgate, Part II (1934)
evidence.locator: Of the Sodein Fal manuscript key, printed pp. 660–661
evidence.summary: Later critical edition directly identifies Trinity R.3.20 pp. 359–361, Harley 2251 old foliation 254r–v, and Additional 29729 fols. 169v–170r.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive complete PDF of MacCracken Part II
evidence.summary: Exact complete PDF used for direct manuscript-key and edition inspection.

evidence.citation: Claire Sponsler, ed., John Lydgate: Mummings and Entertainments (2010)
evidence.summary: Modern scholarly edition and notes support attribution, form, date arguments, performance/display contexts, and manuscript relationships for the three medieval items.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 4108 and 813; manuscript item Chetham6709
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml record-4108 and record-813; data/Manuscripts.xml item Chetham6709; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Versioned source data identifies four Legend witnesses, resolving Chetham 6709 fols. 193r–198v as the fourth, and confirms the three Sodein witnesses. The complete archive also supplies its CC BY 4.0 license.
notes: The cached ZIP is the complete v1.0 release at source commit 5ad5f592868471b44fc72f6e2f42db419780b42c.
Record 4108's R.3.21 locator is internally malformed and record 813's Additional 29729 locus conflicts with MacCracken; repository catalogs and direct edition keys control those mappings.

evidence.citation: Chetham's Library, Middle English Manuscripts
evidence.locator: MS 6709 (A.4.104), Lydgate, Life of Our Lady
evidence.summary: Chetham's official page identifies MS 6709 / A.4.104 as a late-fifteenth-century Lydgate manuscript, states that it was digitized in the Rylands Medieval Collection, and links to high-resolution images.

evidence.citation: Access audit of Chetham MS 6709 high-resolution-images link, 12 July 2026
evidence.summary: The official short link resolves to a legacy LUNA author-results URL. The current service presents a reCAPTCHA verification gate before the inventory, preventing an authorized audit of exact images, target overlap, completeness, and terms without bypassing provider controls.
notes: No automated extraction or CAPTCHA bypass was attempted; no local manuscript image cache was created.

evidence.citation: Chetham's Library, Contact us
evidence.summary: Official page provides librarian and archivist email routes for reader, archive, reproduction, coverage, and reuse enquiries.

evidence.citation: An Account of the European Manuscripts in the Chetham Library, Manchester, entry 6709
evidence.summary: Historical catalog describes the thick quarto vellum-and-paper Lydgate volume, reports contemporary notes dating its copying 1480–1485, and lists the lives of Our Lady and Saints Cecilia, Margaret, George, Edmund, and Fremund.
notes: Use Chetham's current late-fifteenth-century description as the authoritative public summary; retain the narrower historical dates as a qualified reported claim pending direct codex confirmation.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian terms of use
evidence.summary: Provider-wide terms contextualize item-level image licenses, attribution, and reuse conditions.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, Wren Digital Library
evidence.summary: Provider page states image-use context and asks users to seek permission for publication, supplementing item-level CC BY-NC notices.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
evidence.summary: Official duration guidance supports jurisdiction-qualified public-domain analysis for the 1910 and 1911 publications and continued uncertainty for the 1934 edition.

evidence.citation: Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, section 12
evidence.summary: Official UK legislation supplies the ordinary life-plus-seventy literary-work duration rule used for Hammond's article.

open_questions.question: What exact U-M release, download date, and transformation history produced the pinned local CME00085 XML blob?
next_steps: Inspect CME repository history and surviving U-M bulk-distribution documentation.

open_questions.question: Does the Rylands Medieval Collection viewer publish a complete cover-to-cover facsimile of Chetham's Library MS 6709, and does it include the Legend on fols. 193r–198v?
next_steps: Ask Chetham's or the University of Manchester Library for an inventory or direct authorized object link that does not require bypassing the current verification gate.
If a complete public sequence is supplied, cache the whole manuscript, verify first and last physical surfaces and every target folio, and record exact image/canvas mappings.
If the public object is partial, request a complete authorized reproduction of the current physical manuscript and keep the public selection and commissioned copy as distinct facsimile entities.
notes: The witness identity is resolved by DIMEV; only digital-object coverage, access, and reuse remain unresolved.

open_questions.question: Did John Stow copy the Sodein Fal passage in Additional 29729 directly from surviving Trinity R.3.20 or from another Shirley manuscript?
next_steps: Review Sponsler's cited textual evidence and scholarship on Stow's Shirley transcripts.
If necessary compare the exact R.3.20 and Additional 29729 readings after obtaining authorized images of the latter.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply complete authorized digital facsimiles of Harley 2251 and Additional 29729, including all covers, endleaves, blanks, leaves, and edges where available?
next_steps: Use the official catalog enquiry or reproduction route and request each whole manuscript rather than only the target folios.
Ask for exact redistribution, attribution, commercial-use, and repository-publication terms before retaining or publishing delivered files.
Map Sodein Fal separately to Harley current fols. 228r–v / old fols. 254r–v and Additional fols. 169v–170r.
notes: No public copy found does not mean no preservation photography exists.

open_questions.question: Will Bodleian, St John's College, Trinity, and Chetham's/Manchester authorize repository redistribution or commercial print-on-demand use of the manuscript images?
next_steps: Request written permission for any use outside CC BY-NC 4.0, including commercial or profit-making publication.
Keep ignored local research caches separate from distributable repository content until permission is documented.

open_questions.question: What explicit reuse license or permission policy applies to the 2010 Sponsler/METS edition and downloadable PDF?
next_steps: Ask METS or Medieval Institute Publications for terms governing quotation, redistribution, and derivative editions beyond ordinary copyright exceptions.
